info.title: dotnet
info.description: This dotnet tool allows you creating your own dotnet tool based on Microsofts System.CommandLine.Experimental package.

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: ArgumentExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Parse, OK: OptionExtensions.Parse, OK: Parser.Parse, OK: ParserExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: InvocationExtensions.Invoke, OK: InvocationExtensions.Invoke, OK: InvocationExtensions.Invoke, OK: InvocationExtensions.Invoke, OK: InvocationExtensions.Invoke, OK: InvocationExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: InvocationExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: InvocationExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: InvocationExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: InvocationExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription: Run 'dotnet [command] --help' in order to get specific information.

options.name: from-file
options.description: Creates a dotnet tool, from an already serialized tool, which was saved as *.json

options.name: save-to
options.description: Saves the current dotnet tool configuration as json file to given path.

options.name: use-visualstudio
options.description: Opens visual studio after generating the new dotnet tool.

options.name: use-code
options.description: Opens visual studio code after generating with the new dotnet tool.

options.name: use-rider
options.description: Opens the JetBrains Rider IDE after generating with the new dotnet tool.

options.name: as-zip
options.description: Generate a dotnet tool and zip is as zip file.

options.name: use-fastmode
options.description: Do not run MsBuild and no test run of your tool
